discoveredIssues.summary: pg_trgm extension + GIN index for fuzzy search on list_items.content not present; current search is exact-substring only.
discoveredIssues.decision: Defer until fuzzy search becomes a user-validated requirement; do not pre-emptively add a Postgres extension that changes migration baseline + backup/restore drill surface (G115).
discoveredIssues.rationale: Adding pg_trgm is a multi-artifact change (analyst -> design -> plan -> implement) that affects migration baseline, backup ledger, restore drill (G115), and query planning. No user has reported fuzzy-search demand; opportunistic enablement would burn a planning cycle and expand the backup-test matrix for speculative value.
discoveredIssues.trigger: First user-validation signal that exact-substring search is insufficient for /list lookup.

discoveredIssues.summary: GET /api/lists caps limit<=100 and returns {lists,count} with no pagination envelope (cursor or stable offset).
discoveredIssues.decision: Defer pagination envelope decision until a second consumer beyond the web UI lands.
discoveredIssues.rationale: Cursor-vs-offset is a breaking API contract decision requiring analyst + design + consumer impact sweep (web UI, telegram bot, future MCP clients). Today only the web UI consumes the endpoint and the 100-item cap is well above realistic list inventories; premature envelope change would force consumers to rewrite for speculative scale.
discoveredIssues.trigger: Second consumer of GET /api/lists lands (telegram listing, MCP, mobile, or any path that paginates), OR user inventory crosses 100 lists.

discoveredIssues.summary: Per-endpoint auth scopes for list mutation endpoints are not enumerated; handlers rely on upstream chi router policy.
discoveredIssues.decision: Defer until list mutation endpoints are exposed to multi-actor scope (telegram bot writeback, service tokens, MCP).
discoveredIssues.rationale: Picking the scope model (user vs service token, telegram bot shared scope, archive vs delete authority) is a product/policy decision owned by bubbles.analyst. Single-user single-actor today; deferring matches the Smackerel pattern of capability-first design over speculative authz.
discoveredIssues.trigger: Before any mutation endpoint becomes reachable by a second actor (telegram bot writeback, MCP, multi-user scope).

discoveredIssues.summary: list_items.list_id FK ON DELETE behavior unspecified; affects ArchiveList / DeleteList semantics.
discoveredIssues.decision: Defer until ListService.DeleteList or ArchiveList implementation is planned.
discoveredIssues.rationale: FK action change requires a migration that locks list_items and changes archive/delete meaning. Today neither ArchiveList nor DeleteList exists in the handler surface; defining FK behavior in advance constrains the future feature without informing it. Couple the decision to whichever spec authors delete/archive.
discoveredIssues.trigger: Before any spec authoring ListService.DeleteList or ListService.ArchiveList lands its design.md.

executionHistory.summary: Created spec.md, design.md, scopes.md with 8 scopes. Created report.md, uservalidation.md, state.json.

All 8 scopes implemented in internal/list/ (types, store, aggregators, generator), internal/api/ (REST handlers), internal/telegram/ (/list command), internal/intelligence/lists.go (intelligence integration), internal/db/migrations/ (lists tables).
Documentation phase: report.md updated with section evidence; spec/design/scopes synchronized with implementation.
Audit phase: ./smackerel.sh check (config in sync, env_file drift guard OK), ./smackerel.sh lint (Go + Python clean, web manifests OK).

Chaos phase: Honestly skipped — no spec-owned chaos harness exists for the lists path. List generation is deterministic aggregation over annotations + artifacts; failure modes (empty input, nil store, NATS lag) are covered by unit tests.

Manual cross-check: spec.md/design.md/scopes.md aligned with implementation. All 8 scopes Done. List generation pipeline (annotations → aggregator → generator → store → REST/Telegram surfacing) matches spec intent.

Trace-guard closure pass: closed all 40 trace failures (40→0). Added Test Plan tables to Scopes 1-8 pointing at existing tests in internal/list/{types,recipe_aggregator,reading_aggregator,generator}_test.go, internal/api/lists_test.go, internal/telegram/list_test.go, internal/intelligence/lists_test.go (G068 fidelity prefixes added to existing DoD bullets). Appended Trace-Guard Closure section to report.md listing per-scope full repo-relative test paths. No source/test/config/framework changes; no DoD content rewriting (only "Scenario \"<name>\": " prefixes). artifact-lint PASSED, traceability-guard PASSED. No status/certification field changes.

Stochastic-quality-sweep round 5/20 (seed 20260513), child mode test-to-doc. Test probe: ran ./internal/list/..., ./internal/api/... (lists handlers), ./internal/telegram/... (list command), ./internal/intelligence/... (lists subscriber) — all PASS, no flakes. Coverage probe: internal/list 49.7%→51.2% baseline. Mechanical gap closure: added TestRecipeAggregator_InterfaceContract, TestReadingAggregator_InterfaceContract, TestCompareAggregator_InterfaceContract pinning Domain()/DefaultListType() interface contract used by generator.selectAggregator (backs SCN-AL-007/009/010/011). Structural gap (Store.* and PostgresArtifactResolver.* at 0% unit coverage) logged as concern — already covered by tests/integration/artifact_crud_test.go::TestList_CreateAndUpdateStatus and TestList_Chaos_CascadeDeleteDuringConcurrentUpdates per design (live-stack boundary). Verification: all tests green, go vet clean. No status/certification field changes; spec remains done.
